header.title: TCO car simulator
header.subtitle: To see if switching to electric pays off: compare running costs (energy, maintenance, insurance), financing, and resale value over the same period. Typical use case: your current ICE / diesel vs the electric vehicle you are considering.
header.horizonHint: Period used for the yearly table, chart, and depreciation (purchase − resale). Independent of loan length, which is set under Financing.

financing.title: Financing
financing.intro: Loan length is the same for both vehicles: monthly payments during that period, then €0. It can differ from the comparison horizon (set under the vehicle names). Without a loan: the “vehicle” share is straight-line depreciation over the horizon (from Buy / sell amounts).

purchase.title: Purchase & resale
purchase.intro: For the electric side, enter the purchase price and estimated resale after {years} years. For ICE, you often already own the car: resale at the horizon is the key input; current value spreads depreciation over the period (and any loan).
purchase.fieldMicro: Used for month-by-month depreciation and loan principal on this vehicle.
purchase.netVehicleHint: Net “vehicle” cost over the horizon (purchase or current value − resale):

mileage.title: Mileage
mileage.intro: One input mode at a time, using the same yearly bounds ({kmMin}–{kmMax} km/year). In trip mode, EV and ICE share the same annual mileage. In direct mode, you can set them separately.
mileage.readoutApprox: ≈ {kmMonth} km / month · {kmYear} km / year (same for both vehicles)

results.verdictLabel: Summary over {y} years (costs: energy, maintenance, insurance, loan or depreciation)
results.verdictClose: Costs are very close over the period
results.gapNegligible: Negligible gap between the two scenarios.
results.usageNote: Energy + maintenance + insurance, no loan or purchase depreciation.
results.equityNote: Linear resale value − remaining loan (if any). Positive = stronger position for the {ev} at resale.
results.chartLegendCumulative: Cumulative gap (ICE − EV) — above 0 means an EV advantage

yearly.title: Year-by-year detail ({n} years)
yearly.legendCost: Cost {ev} / {ice}: energy + maintenance + insurance + loan payments or straight-line depreciation (purchase − resale) over the chosen horizon.
yearly.legendDelta: Δ / yr: ICE cost − electric cost. Positive = this year the EV is cheaper (gain). Negative = the EV is more expensive (vs ICE).
yearly.legendCumul: Cumulative Δ: sum of yearly Δ — same sign: cumulative advantage for electric if positive.
yearly.footerEvWins: With your assumptions, the {ev} has total charges {amount} € lower than the {ice} over the period.
yearly.footerIceWins: With your assumptions, the {ice} has total charges {amount} € lower than the {ev} over the period.
yearly.footerClose: The two scenarios are very close in total charges over the period.
yearly.footerSub: Cumulative totals: {totalEv} € ({ev}) · {totalIce} € ({ice}). Estimated resale equity (value − loan): {eqEv} € vs {eqIce} €.
